message:
test(readme): TypeError variant triggers (Lossy/InvalidBool/IncompatibleDtypes)

TypeError has 10+ variants but only IncompatibleDtypes was constructed
in the prelude completeness compile guard. The other variants are
documented failure modes that should route to specific enum variants
on bad input — but no test asserted the routing was correct. A
regression that conflated InvalidCast and LossyFloatToInt would not
have been caught.

Adds readme_typeerror_variant_triggers — reachable via prelude alone —
that pattern-matches the specific error variant returned by:

- cast_scalar(Float64(3.14), Int64) → Err(LossyFloatToInt{value:3.14})
- cast_scalar(Int64(2), Bool)       → Err(InvalidBoolInt{value:2})
- cast_scalar(Float64(0.5), Bool)   → Err(InvalidBoolFloat{value:0.5})
- common_dtype(Utf8, Bool)          → Err(IncompatibleDtypes{left:Utf8, right:Bool})

Each matches! includes the inner field value, so a regression that
returned the right variant with the wrong payload still fails the
test.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.41.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
